summary: Martin Fowler introduces Sergey Dmitriev's Meta Programming System (MPS), an exploratory tool from JetBrains that exemplifies what Dmitriev calls Language Oriented Programming. Fowler reports being impressed after a hands-on demonstration of the system. He situates MPS within a broader emerging movement focused on building software through integrated sets of domain-specific languages. The post briefly notes other players in this space including Intentional Programming and Microsoft's Whitehorse work.
key_points: JetBrains' Sergey Dmitriev has developed the Meta Programming System as an example of Language Oriented Programming
The approach involves building software through closely integrated sets of domain-specific languages
Other notable players in this space include Intentional Programming and Microsoft's Whitehorse work
Fowler signals this as an important area to watch in coming years
money_quotes: I'm very interested in this growing movement, one that looks to build software through closely integrated sets of DomainSpecificLanguages.
It's an area to keep your eye on over the next few years.
key_insight: Language Oriented Programming, which builds software through integrated domain-specific languages, represents an emerging movement worth watching closely.
